claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

First, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm inquiring about. Their name is Alex, and they're 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 13 years old.

Now I need to ask: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't currently live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, there are specific circumstances where you may still be eligible for Child Benefit.

Could you please tell me: **Which country are you currently living in, and are you or your partner a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of the armed forces posted abroad)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not a Crown servant, no. I'm not a diplomat or in the armed forces - just living abroad as a regular civilian.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. 

To help determine your eligibility, I need to understand more about your situation. **Are you living in an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or are you in a different country?** And if you don't mind sharing, what is the reason you're living abroad (for example, work, study, or other reasons)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not get into all the specific details about where exactly I am or why. Is that information absolutely necessary? I'm just trying to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for Alex even though I'm not in the UK right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand your concern about privacy. However, I do need to be honest with you about the eligibility rules.

According to the Child Benefit rules, you **normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK**.

There are only specific exceptions for people living abroad:
- Crown servants (diplomats, armed forces posted abroad, etc.)
- People living in certain specific countries with particular agreements with the UK

Since you've confirmed you're not a Crown servant and you don't currently live in the UK, **unfortunately, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex unless you fall within those very specific exceptions for certain countries.**

Without knowing which country you're in, I cannot definitively confirm whether one of those country-specific exceptions might apply to you. If you're willing to share which country you're residing in, I can give you a more precise answer. Otherwise, based on the general rule that you must live in the UK, you would not be eligible.

Would you like to share which country you're in so I can check if an exception applies?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, that's alright. I understand what you're saying - basically since I'm not in the UK and I'm not a Crown servant, I won't be eligible for Child Benefit. That's clear enough. Thank you for explaining the rules to me.
